title: portulaca cyclophylla
intro: Portulaca cyclophylla, also known as Indian purslane, is a succulent plant species native to India and Southeast Asia, characterized by its thick, fleshy leaves and stems. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Portulaca cyclophylla include reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and aiding in digestion, making it a valuable herb for overall well-being. Portulaca cyclophylla can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and powders, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
